data.description:
## Context

Two CI workflows are persistently failing on main: ticket-platform-matrix.yml (multi-platform matrix: linux-bash4, macos-bash3, alpine-busybox) and ticket-perf-regression.yml (depends on hyperfine installer). Their persistent red status appears to have desensitized the team — they no longer act as gates. These are exactly the workflows that would surface Pattern 1 (hermeticity), Pattern 4 (SIGPIPE on Linux), and Pattern 8 (multi-platform / perf failures) regressions.

Separately, scripts/release.sh precondition #8 (validate.sh --ci) is the de facto integration test — 16 of 22 recent closed bugs were detected here, days after the regressions landed. The pre-commit hook stack admits these regressions because none of the hooks are environmental.

This epic codifies CI health as a treated-as-outage signal, hardens the release gate against single-pass-after-red-streak, and ships an opt-in post-commit feedback mechanism so regressions surface immediately at the next interaction rather than at release time.

See docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md Pattern 8 and docs/process-gaps-4-26.md G7, G8.

## Cited evidence

Pattern 8 — CI-only multi-platform / perf failures (verified via gh run list):
- ticket-platform-matrix.yml: 7/7 of last 7 runs failed (100%), all on main, dates 2026-04-24 through 2026-04-26
- ticket-perf-regression.yml: 5/5 of last 5 runs failed (100%), all on main
- Matrix legs declared at .github/workflows/ticket-platform-matrix.yml:35-46: linux-bash4, macos-bash3, alpine-busybox
- Triggers (per .github/workflows/ticket-platform-matrix.yml:6-18): push to main AND a path-filtered pull_request trigger (filter so narrow that most relevant changes don't fire it). Even if green, would not gate most PRs.
- No open ticket clearly tracking the persistent red status as a CI-infrastructure bug; silently tolerated

By contrast (healthy CI workflows): template-real-url-e2e.yml, inbound-bridge.yml, outbound-bridge.yml, ci-python-skills.yml, portability-smoke.yml, ticket-lifecycle.yml, ci.yml (5/5 last runs success). The two broken workflows are exactly those that test cross-platform / performance behavior.

G7 — Release-gate as integration test (verified):
- scripts/release.sh:179-183 confirmed: precondition #8 invokes .claude/scripts/dso validate.sh --ci, aborts release on failure
- Closed-bug discovery channel breakdown: CI/release gate = 17 of 37; debug-everything = 15; code review = 3; user-flagged = 2 (from docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md)
- The pre-commit gate stack contains no equivalent of validate.sh --ci. pre-commit-test-gate.sh is per-staged-file, not full-suite.

G8 — No tracking ticket exists: verified by absence — dso ticket list --status=open does not include workflow-health tickets for these two CI workflows.

## Mitigations included

- G7-M1 — Post-commit hook (or .git/hooks/post-commit) triggers validate.sh --ci in the background after every commit on a worktree branch; results piped to a notification channel (terminal banner, file in ~/.claude/logs/). Failures do not block the commit (post-commit hooks cannot), but alert the orchestrator at next interaction. Opt-in via dso-config.conf (background_validate.enabled, default false).
- G8-M1 — File two P1 bug tickets immediately for the persistent-red workflows (one per workflow, tagged ci-infrastructure), with failure logs as evidence. Add to CLAUDE.md "Always Do These": "When a CI workflow fails 3+ consecutive times on main, file a P1 ticket within 24 hours. Persistent CI red is treated as an outage, not a known-issue."
- G8-M2 — Extend scripts/release.sh precondition checklist with: "For each workflow declared as required in release-required-workflows.yaml: assert the last 3 runs on main are all green. A single green run after a red streak is not sufficient." Prevents flaky-pass from unblocking a release that should be held.

## Proposed success criteria

1. Two P1 tickets exist tagged ci-infrastructure referencing the failure logs of ticket-platform-matrix.yml and ticket-perf-regression.yml; each has a clear root-cause hypothesis and proposed fix path
2. CLAUDE.md "Always Do These" amended with the 3+ consecutive failure rule, citing the empirical context (this epic and the cited gh run data)
3. release-required-workflows.yaml created at the repo root listing workflows whose health blocks release
4. scripts/release.sh precondition #8 extended (or new precondition added) requiring last-3-green per declared workflow; release aborts on any red within the window
5. Post-commit hook is opt-in via dso-config.conf (background_validate.enabled, default false); when enabled, surfaces results via existing notification channel without blocking commit
6. The ticket-platform-matrix.yml and ticket-perf-regression.yml failures are diagnosed and resolved as part of this epic's scope (the P1 tickets in SC1 are children of this epic, not separate work)
7. Test coverage: a fixture asserts the release-streak check correctly aborts when a workflow has any red in the last 3 runs, and proceeds when all 3 are green

## Coordination notes

- Pairs with the items absorbed by cf7b-86a9 (G1-M3, G9-M1, G9-M2) and 3aaa-0238 (G7-M3, G8-M3, G12-M3): collectively the four epics close the local-vs-CI parity gap from multiple angles
- The release-streak requirement (G8-M2) is upstream-compatible with 1083-fb3d's PR-based merge model — once PRs are mandatory, the streak check applies to PR-required workflows
- SC6 (diagnose the two persistent-red workflows) has unknown depth; planning may need to split SC6 into a separate sub-epic if root-cause investigation reveals significant infrastructure work

## References

- docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md Pattern 8 and cross-cutting observations on release-gate-as-integration-test
- docs/process-gaps-4-26.md G7, G8 (verified via gh run list)
- docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md G7-M1, G8-M1, G8-M2
- docs/new-epics-4-26.md § New Epic F
data.title: CI health enforcement and release-gate streak requirement
